lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah. Blah, blah, blah, blah...blah!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah. Blah, blah, blah, blah...blah!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Well, I never expected to be impressed by a human, but you have done a good deed for the gnomes - even if most gnomes won't recognise it. You humans are not as stupid as I first thought...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, that's probably as close to a compliment I'm ever likely to get from you, so thanks!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: So you want to know what your crystal seed does?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you.. ? Oh I forgot about this mind meld thing.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: The seed is a piece of elven magic, all elven magic comes from the manipulation of seeds of crystal. The elves give it divine properties. Elves sing to their crystals and they form into the desired shape.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how do I turn it into something useful?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Either talk to an elf or find some other way to sing the crystal. If this came from Oaknock, he must have found a way...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'll have a look round Oaknock's old workshop. I can have a chat with Brimstail while I'm at it! Thanks!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: So you want to know what your crystal saw does?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did you.. ? Oh I forgot about this mind meld thing.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Well, I do remember seeing Oaknock using the saw. He said it allowed him to build devices more complicated that even he was capable of constructing. The device he built must have been very complicated indeed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd say! Anyway, the saw looks like it could be pretty useful. Thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Hazelmere.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: You're back. Come to disturb me, have you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, good old Hazelmere. Glad to see you're back to your grumpy self.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Do you actually want something from me, or are you simply making my life a misery again?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I just wanted to have a chat. You know, just check how you're doing.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Well I'm fine, thank you. Now leave me in peace.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah, blah?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah, blah, blah!

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah, blah, blah...blah!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: Blah, blah.

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: *Hazelmere nods his thanks*

lines.speaker: Hazelmere
lines.text: You must have had to travel a long way to get this to me. Here's a little something extra as a tip.
